protocol.raw_hid:
RAW_EPSIZE / report size: 32 bytes. The keyboard's raw HID handler is void raw_hid_receive(uint8_t *data, uint8_t length) (quantum/via.c:279) and it always echoes the full buffer back with raw_hid_send(data, length) (quantum/via.c:461). In QMK core RAW_EPSIZE == 32, so every transaction is a fixed 32-byte report in and a 32-byte report out. There is no length prefix; unused trailing bytes are simply ignored on receive and echoed/garbage on response.

PAYLOAD vs reportID (macOS / IOKit): On macOS, IOHIDDevice / IOHIDDeviceSetReport prepends the HID report ID as the first byte of the transfer. QMK raw HID uses report ID 0 (no numbered reports), so the host must send a 33-byte transfer = [0x00 reportID] + [32-byte payload], OR use the IOKit API in the mode where the report ID is passed as a separate argument and the data buffer is the 32-byte payload. The KEY POINT for the implementer: the firmware's data[] is the 32-byte payload only. data[0] = command id (one of enum via_command_id), data[1..31] = command_data (command_data = &data[1], quantum/via.c:281). So payload[0] = command id, and on IOKit you must add the leading reportID 0 byte that is NOT part of the 32-byte payload the firmware sees. When reading the response, strip the leading report-ID byte (if your read path includes it) to recover the 32-byte payload; data[0] of the response is the (possibly id_unhandled=0xFF) command id echo.

Endianness of multi-byte keycodes: BIG-ENDIAN, high byte first. A 16-bit keycode is always [high_byte, low_byte]. See dynamic_keymap_get_keycode setting command_data[3]=keycode>>8, command_data[4]=keycode&0xFF (quantum/via.c:372-373) and set_keycode reading (command_data[3]<<8)|command_data[4] (quantum/via.c:377). EEPROM itself stores keycodes big-endian (dynamic_keymap.c:127-129, 146-148; dynamic_keymap.h:31 "Data is big-endian 16-bit values"), so buffer reads/writes (get_buffer/set_buffer) return/accept raw big-endian keycode bytes. Encoders are likewise big-endian (dynamic_keymap.c:160-171). Multi-byte 32-bit values (uptime, layout_options, firmware_version) are also big-endian, MSB first (quantum/via.c:298-311, 333-337).

16-bit length/size and offset fields are ALSO big-endian: protocol version is command_data[0]=ver>>8, command_data[1]=ver&0xFF (quantum/via.c:291-292); macro buffer size is command_data[0]=size>>8, command_data[1]=size&0xFF (quantum/via.c:403-404); all buffer offsets are read as offset=(command_data[0]<<8)|command_data[1] (quantum/via.c:408, 414, 428, 434).

get_buffer / set_buffer chunking (offset + size): All four buffer commands (dynamic_keymap get/set buffer 0x12/0x13, macro get/set buffer 0x0E/0x0F) use the same wire layout. command_data[0..1] = 16-bit BIG-ENDIAN byte offset into the target buffer; command_data[2] = size (uint8, number of bytes this chunk transfers); command_data[3..] = the data bytes (read into or written from &command_data[3]). Because the report payload is 32 bytes and the header occupies payload[0]=cmd, payload[1..2]=offset, payload[3]=size, the data window is payload[4..31] = 28 bytes max. Hence size <= 28 (the firmware literally comments "size <= 28" at quantum/via.c:409, 415, 429, 435). To transfer a whole keymap or macro buffer, the host loops: chunk k uses offset = k*28, size = min(28, remaining), reading/writing 28 bytes per raw HID transfer. Note offset is in BYTES, and since keycodes are 2 bytes each, 28 bytes = 14 keycodes per transfer (dynamic_keymap.h:35-37). On get_buffer, bytes past the end of the real buffer are zero-filled (dynamic_keymap.c:210-214); on set_buffer, writes past the end are silently dropped (dynamic_keymap.c:224-228).

Indexing note for keymap buffer: byte offset for (layer,row,col) = (layer*MATRIX_ROWS*MATRIX_COLS*2) + (row*MATRIX_COLS*2) + (col*2) (dynamic_keymap.c:115-118; dynamic_keymap.h:32-34). Total keymap buffer size = DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_LAYER_COUNT * MATRIX_ROWS * MATRIX_COLS * 2 (dynamic_keymap.c:206).

commands.name: id_get_protocol_version
commands.purpose: Return the VIA protocol version so the host (VIA Configurator) can confirm compatible firmware. Here VIA_PROTOCOL_VERSION = 0x000C (quantum/via.h:62).
commands.request: REQUEST (32-byte payload): payload[0]=0x01 (command id). payload[1..31]=don't care. Defined at quantum/via.h:75, handled at quantum/via.c:290-294.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0 (payload[0]) = 0x01 (command id echoed). byte1 (command_data[0]) = version high byte = VIA_PROTOCOL_VERSION>>8 = 0x00. byte2 (command_data[1]) = version low byte = VIA_PROTOCOL_VERSION&0xFF = 0x0C. So a v12 keyboard returns [0x01, 0x00, 0x0C, ...]. Big-endian 16-bit. quantum/via.c:291-292.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_get_keycode
commands.purpose: Read the 16-bit keycode at (layer,row,column) from the dynamic keymap in EEPROM.
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x04; payload[1] (command_data[0])=layer; payload[2] (command_data[1])=row; payload[3] (command_data[2])=column. quantum/via.h:78, quantum/via.c:370-374. Underlying read dynamic_keymap.c:120-133 (returns KC_NO=0x0000 if layer/row/col out of range).
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x04; bytes1-3 = layer,row,column echoed (unchanged); byte4 (command_data[3]) = keycode high byte (keycode>>8); byte5 (command_data[4]) = keycode low byte (keycode&0xFF). Big-endian. quantum/via.c:371-373.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_set_keycode
commands.purpose: Write a 16-bit keycode to (layer,row,column) in the dynamic keymap EEPROM.
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x05; payload[1] (command_data[0])=layer; payload[2] (command_data[1])=row; payload[3] (command_data[2])=column; payload[4] (command_data[3])=keycode high byte; payload[5] (command_data[4])=keycode low byte. Firmware reconstructs keycode=(command_data[3]<<8)|command_data[4] (big-endian). quantum/via.h:79, quantum/via.c:376-378. Underlying write dynamic_keymap.c:135-150 (no-op if out of range).
commands.response: RESPONSE: full 32-byte buffer echoed unchanged (byte0=0x05, layer/row/col/keycode bytes as sent). No extra data is written by the handler; it just acknowledges by echo. quantum/via.c:461.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_get_buffer
commands.purpose: Bulk-read a chunk of the raw dynamic keymap EEPROM buffer (big-endian keycodes, ordered layer/row/column). Lets host read up to 14 keycodes (28 bytes) per transfer.
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x12; payload[1..2] (command_data[0..1]) = 16-bit BIG-ENDIAN byte offset = (command_data[0]<<8)|command_data[1]; payload[3] (command_data[2]) = size (bytes, <=28); payload[4..] = don't care. quantum/via.h:92, quantum/via.c:427-431. Underlying read dynamic_keymap.c:205-218.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x12; bytes1-3 = offset_hi, offset_lo, size echoed; bytes4.. (&command_data[3]) = 'size' bytes read from EEPROM at DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_EEPROM_ADDR+offset. Bytes beyond the real keymap size (LAYER_COUNT*ROWS*COLS*2) are returned as 0x00. quantum/via.c:430, dynamic_keymap.c:209-217.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_set_buffer
commands.purpose: Bulk-write a chunk of the raw dynamic keymap EEPROM buffer (big-endian keycodes). Up to 28 bytes (14 keycodes) per transfer.
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x13; payload[1..2] (command_data[0..1]) = 16-bit BIG-ENDIAN byte offset; payload[3] (command_data[2]) = size (bytes, <=28); payload[4..(4+size-1)] (&command_data[3]) = the data bytes to write. quantum/via.h:93, quantum/via.c:433-437. Underlying write dynamic_keymap.c:220-231 (writes past the keymap size are silently dropped).
commands.response: RESPONSE: full 32-byte buffer echoed unchanged (byte0=0x13, offset/size/data as sent). Acknowledge-by-echo only. quantum/via.c:461.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_get_layer_count
commands.purpose: Return the number of dynamic keymap layers (DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_LAYER_COUNT, default 4, dynamic_keymap.c:39-41).
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x11; payload[1..31]=don't care. quantum/via.h:91, quantum/via.c:423-426.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x11; byte1 (command_data[0]) = layer count (e.g. 0x04). Single byte. quantum/via.c:424, dynamic_keymap.c:111-113.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_get_encoder
commands.purpose: Read the 16-bit keycode bound to an encoder rotation. Only compiled when ENCODER_MAP_ENABLE is defined (quantum/via.c:439-445). If not enabled, this id falls through to default and returns id_unhandled (0xFF) in byte0.
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x14; payload[1] (command_data[0])=layer; payload[2] (command_data[1])=encoder_id; payload[3] (command_data[2])=direction: 0 = counter-clockwise, non-zero (1) = clockwise (firmware uses command_data[2]!=0). quantum/via.h:94, quantum/via.c:440-444. Underlying read dynamic_keymap.c:157-164 (KC_NO if out of range).
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x14; bytes1-3 = layer,encoder_id,direction echoed; byte4 (command_data[3]) = keycode high byte; byte5 (command_data[4]) = keycode low byte. Big-endian. quantum/via.c:441-443.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_set_encoder
commands.purpose: Write the 16-bit keycode for an encoder rotation. Only compiled when ENCODER_MAP_ENABLE is defined; otherwise returns id_unhandled (0xFF).
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x15; payload[1] (command_data[0])=layer; payload[2] (command_data[1])=encoder_id; payload[3] (command_data[2])=direction (0=CCW, non-zero=CW); payload[4] (command_data[3])=keycode high byte; payload[5] (command_data[4])=keycode low byte. keycode=(command_data[3]<<8)|command_data[4], big-endian. quantum/via.h:95, quantum/via.c:446-448. Underlying write dynamic_keymap.c:166-172.
commands.response: RESPONSE: full 32-byte buffer echoed unchanged (byte0=0x15, params as sent). Acknowledge-by-echo only. quantum/via.c:461.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_macro_get_count
commands.purpose: Return the maximum number of macros that can be stored (DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_MACRO_COUNT, default 16, dynamic_keymap.c:43-45). NOTE: this is the capacity, not the current count of stored macros (dynamic_keymap.h:61-62).
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x0C; payload[1..31]=don't care. quantum/via.h:86, quantum/via.c:397-400.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x0C; byte1 (command_data[0]) = macro count (e.g. 0x10 for 16). Single byte. quantum/via.c:398, dynamic_keymap.c:260-262.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_macro_get_buffer_size
commands.purpose: Return the total size in bytes of the macro EEPROM buffer (DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_MACRO_EEPROM_SIZE).
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x0D; payload[1..31]=don't care. quantum/via.h:87, quantum/via.c:401-405.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x0D; byte1 (command_data[0]) = size high byte (size>>8); byte2 (command_data[1]) = size low byte (size&0xFF). 16-bit BIG-ENDIAN. quantum/via.c:403-404, dynamic_keymap.c:264-266.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_macro_get_buffer
commands.purpose: Bulk-read a chunk of the macro buffer (list of null-terminated SS_* macro strings). Up to 28 bytes per transfer.
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x0E; payload[1..2] (command_data[0..1]) = 16-bit BIG-ENDIAN byte offset; payload[3] (command_data[2]) = size (bytes, <=28); payload[4..]=don't care. quantum/via.h:88, quantum/via.c:407-412. Underlying read dynamic_keymap.c:268-280.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x0E; bytes1-3 = offset_hi, offset_lo, size echoed; bytes4.. (&command_data[3]) = 'size' bytes read from macro EEPROM at DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_MACRO_EEPROM_ADDR+offset. Bytes past DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_MACRO_EEPROM_SIZE are returned as 0x00. quantum/via.c:410, dynamic_keymap.c:272-278.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_macro_set_buffer
commands.purpose: Bulk-write a chunk of the macro buffer. Up to 28 bytes per transfer. Host protocol: the macro buffer holds null-terminated strings; to safely write, host should first set the LAST byte of the buffer to non-zero (e.g. 0xFF) to mark in-progress, write all chunks, then set the last byte to 0x00 when done (dynamic_keymap.h:44-56). macro_send refuses to run while the last byte is non-zero (dynamic_keymap.c:308-315).
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x0F; payload[1..2] (command_data[0..1]) = 16-bit BIG-ENDIAN byte offset; payload[3] (command_data[2]) = size (bytes, <=28); payload[4..(4+size-1)] (&command_data[3]) = data bytes to write. quantum/via.h:89, quantum/via.c:413-417. Underlying write dynamic_keymap.c:282-292 (writes past buffer size silently dropped).
commands.response: RESPONSE: full 32-byte buffer echoed unchanged (byte0=0x0F, offset/size/data as sent). Acknowledge-by-echo only. quantum/via.c:461.

commands.name: id_dynamic_keymap_macro_reset
commands.purpose: Clear the entire macro buffer (zero-fill all DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_MACRO_EEPROM_SIZE bytes).
commands.request: REQUEST: payload[0]=0x10; payload[1..31]=don't care. quantum/via.h:90, quantum/via.c:419-422. Underlying dynamic_keymap.c:294-301.
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x10; rest of buffer echoed unchanged. Acknowledge-by-echo only (no data written by handler). quantum/via.c:461.

commands.name: id_custom_get_value
commands.purpose: Read a 'custom value' for a sub-feature channel (lighting, audio, etc.). Routed by channel id to the matching handler; if the channel/value is unknown or the feature is not compiled in, byte0 is set to id_unhandled (0xFF).
commands.request: REQUEST: data = [command_id, channel_id, value_id, value_data...]. payload[0]=0x08; payload[1] (command_data[0])=channel_id (enum via_channel_id, quantum/via.h:107-114: 0=custom,1=qmk_backlight,2=qmk_rgblight,3=qmk_rgb_matrix,4=qmk_audio,5=qmk_led_matrix); payload[2] (command_data[1])=value_id (channel-specific, e.g. brightness=1, effect=2, effect_speed=3, color=4; quantum/via.h:116-144); payload[3..] = value_data placeholder (filled in response). Dispatch: quantum/via.c:384-389 -> via_custom_value_command (225-270) -> channel handler -> get_value reads value_id_and_data = &data[2].
commands.response: RESPONSE: byte0=0x08 (or 0xFF if unhandled); byte1=channel_id echoed; byte2=value_id echoed; byte3.. (&data[3], i.e. value_data after value_id) = the value bytes. Most values are 1 byte at byte3 (e.g. brightness, effect, speed). 'color' returns 2 bytes: byte3=hue, byte4=sat (e.g. quantum/via.c:592-595 rgblight, 680-684 rgb_matrix). Audio enable/clicky return 1 byte 0/1 (855-868). For rgb/led matrix, effect getter returns 0 when disabled else current mode (672-674, 784-786).

commands.name: id_custom_set_value
commands.purpose: Write a 'custom value' for a sub-feature channel (applied immediately, _noeeprom variants - not persisted until id_custom_save). Unknown channel/value or disabled feature => byte0=id_unhandled (0xFF).
commands.request: REQUEST: data = [command_id, channel_id, value_id, value_data...]. payload[0]=0x07; payload[1] (command_data[0])=channel_id; payload[2] (command_data[1])=value_id; payload[3..] = value_data: 1 byte for brightness/effect/speed/enable; for 'color' 2 bytes (byte3=hue, byte4=sat). Dispatch quantum/via.c:384-389 -> via_custom_value_command (225-270) -> channel handler -> set_value operates on value_id_and_data=&data[2] (e.g. 516-537 backlight, 600-627 rgblight, 688-716 rgb_matrix, 795-818 led_matrix, 871-885 audio).
commands.response: RESPONSE: full 32-byte buffer echoed unchanged (byte0=0x07 unless unhandled=0xFF; channel_id/value_id/value_data as sent). Acknowledge-by-echo. quantum/via.c:461.

commands.name: id_custom_save
commands.purpose: Persist the current custom values for a channel to EEPROM (e.g. eeconfig_update_*). Unknown channel or disabled feature => byte0=id_unhandled (0xFF).
commands.request: REQUEST: data = [command_id, channel_id, ...]. payload[0]=0x09; payload[1] (command_data[0])=channel_id (which sub-feature to save). value_id is not required for save. Dispatch quantum/via.c:384-389 -> via_custom_value_command (225-270) -> channel handler's save case (e.g. backlight 480-483/539-541, rgblight 564-566/629-631, rgb_matrix 651-653/718-743, led_matrix 763-765/820-822, audio 844-846/887-889).
commands.response: RESPONSE: full 32-byte buffer echoed unchanged (byte0=0x09 unless unhandled=0xFF). Acknowledge-by-echo. quantum/via.c:461.

protocol.notes:
Source files (all absolute):
- /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/quantum/via.c — raw_hid_receive command dispatch (switch at lines 289-457), echo-back at line 461; custom-value routing via_custom_value_command (225-270) and per-channel handlers (backlight 466+, rgblight 550+, rgb_matrix 637+, led_matrix 749+, audio 830+).
- /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/quantum/via.h — command id enum (74-97), VIA_PROTOCOL_VERSION 0x000C (line 62), keyboard-value ids (99-105), channel ids (107-114), per-channel value ids (116-144).
- /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/quantum/dynamic_keymap.c — keycode/encoder/buffer/macro implementations and EEPROM layout (lines 111-386); big-endian storage at 127-129, 146-148, 160-171.
- /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/quantum/dynamic_keymap.h — function prototypes and the big-endian / buffer-chunking / macro-valid-flag protocol notes (lines 30-68).

CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION FACTS:
1. Report size is 32 bytes (RAW_EPSIZE in QMK core). Every request is a 32-byte payload; the firmware always replies by echoing the same 32-byte buffer, mutated in place (raw_hid_send(data,length), via.c:461). There is no separate ACK/length field.
2. On macOS IOKit, prepend reportID 0: the on-wire transfer is [0x00] + 32-byte payload, but firmware-visible data[0] = command id. command_data = &data[1] (via.c:281), so payload offsets in my command layouts are relative to the 32-byte payload (payload[0]=cmd).
3. ALL multi-byte integers are BIG-ENDIAN, high byte first: 16-bit keycodes, encoder keycodes, protocol version, macro buffer size, and the 16-bit offset field in every buffer command; 32-bit values (uptime/layout_options/firmware_version) too.
4. Buffer chunking (ids 0x0E,0x0F,0x12,0x13): payload[1..2]=16-bit BE byte offset, payload[3]=size (<=28 because the data window is payload[4..31] = 28 bytes), data at payload[4..]. Loop with offset += 28 per transfer. 28 bytes = 14 keycodes. get_buffer zero-fills past end; set_buffer drops writes past end.
5. Unknown command id, unknown keyboard/custom value, or a feature that is not compiled in => the handler sets data[0] = id_unhandled = 0xFF (via.h:96). So the host detects "unsupported" by checking response byte0 == 0xFF (rather than == request id).
6. Encoder commands (0x14/0x15) only exist when ENCODER_MAP_ENABLE is compiled in; otherwise they hit the default branch and return 0xFF in byte0.
7. Macro write protocol: set last buffer byte non-zero (0xFF) before writing, write chunks via 0x0F, then set last byte to 0x00. macro_send aborts while last byte != 0 (dynamic_keymap.c:308-315). Macro strings are null-terminated; SS_QMK_PREFIX (0x01) introduces tap/down/up/delay codes (dynamic_keymap.c:344-383).
8. macro_get_count (0x0C) and get_layer_count (0x11) each return a single byte at command_data[0] (payload byte1). macro_get_count returns capacity (default 16), NOT the number of macros actually stored.

Other ids present in the firmware but not requested: id_get_keyboard_value=0x02 (sub-ids uptime/layout_options/switch_matrix_state/firmware_version/device_indication), id_set_keyboard_value=0x03 (layout_options, device_indication), id_dynamic_keymap_reset=0x06, id_eeprom_reset=0x0A (only if VIA_EEPROM_ALLOW_RESET defined), id_bootloader_jump=0x0B (declared in enum at via.h:85 but NOT handled in this raw_hid_receive switch, so it returns 0xFF here).

encoders.note: Single rotary encoder (knob). pin_a=B15, pin_b=B14, ENCODER_DEFAULT_POS=0x3. encoder_map enabled. Default action per layer: MAC_BASE/WIN_BASE = ENCODER_CCW_CW(KC_VOLD, KC_VOLU) i.e. volume down/up; MAC_FN/WIN_FN = ENCODER_CCW_CW(RGB_VAD, RGB_VAI) i.e. RGB brightness down/up. The knob's PUSH (press) is a separate matrix switch at row0,col15 (KC_MUTE on base, RGB_TOG on Fn), already included as a physical key above.

layout.notes:
SOURCES: physical layout + matrix from /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/keyboards/keychron/v1_max/ansi_encoder/info.json (only one layout macro: LAYOUT_ansi_82, 82 keys). Matrix dims from /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/keyboards/keychron/v1_max/info.json matrix_pins: rows=[C12,D2,B3,B4,B5,B6] -> 6 rows; cols=[C6,C7,C8,A14,A15,C10,C11,C13,C14,C15,C0,C1,C2,C3,A0,A1] -> 16 cols. diode_direction=ROW2COL.

LAYER COUNT: DYNAMIC_KEYMAP_LAYER_COUNT is NOT defined anywhere in v1_max (grep returned nothing in config.h/rules.mk), so QMK's compile-time default of 4 applies. This matches the default keymap.c which defines exactly 4 layers: MAC_BASE, MAC_FN, WIN_BASE, WIN_FN. So layer_count=4 with high confidence.

HOW LAYOUT ENCODES MATRIX: each entry in layouts.LAYOUT_ansi_82.layout is one physical key with explicit "matrix":[row,col]. x/y/w/h are key units (1u default; w/h omitted means 1). The layout array order is positional and maps 1:1 (left-to-right, top-to-bottom) onto the keycodes in each LAYOUT_ansi_82(...) call in keymap.c. NOTE: matrix columns are NON-CONTIGUOUS per row because the LAYOUT macro skips unused electrical positions — e.g. row0 jumps col13 -> col15 (col14 unused on row0); row1/row2 jump col13 -> col15; row3 skips col12 (Enter occupies matrix col13 but spans the col12 cap position) and jumps to col15; row4 skips col1 (2.25u left Shift occupies col0, next key is col2) and uses col14 for the Up arrow (no col13/col15 in row4); row5 uses cols 0,1,2 then jumps to col6 (6.25u Space) then 10,11,12,13,14,15. The encoder knob press is the switch at matrix[0,15].

LABELS: legends are derived from the MAC_BASE (default/first) layer of keymap.c since info.json carries no legend text. Modifier/media keys are given human-readable caps (Cmd/Opt for KC_LCMMD/KC_LOPTN, etc.). The two leftmost-column row0 keys after Esc are macOS BrtDn/BrtUp; MissionCtrl=KC_MCTRL, Launchpad=KC_LNPAD. RGB_VAD/RGB_VAI kept as keycode names (RGB value down/up). matrix[0,15] cap = KC_MUTE on base layer (the encoder press), labeled 'Mute (knob)'. The 'Fn (MO)' key at row5,col11 is MO(MAC_FN). Total = 82 keys, matching LAYOUT_ansi_82.

CAVEAT on Enter (row3,col13): in info.json it sits at x=12.75 spanning w=2.25, so the cap visually covers the col11(') -> col13 region; there is no separate col12 entry in row3. Backspace (row1,col13) w=2 at x=13. These wide keys are single matrix switches.

keycodes.layer_keycode_encoding:
All layer keycodes are built by OR-ing (here equivalent to adding) a base range value with the layer number. Values are 16-bit; cite keycodes.h enum qk_keycode_ranges (lines 41-54). Layer number must fit in the range width (most are 5 bits, 0..31; LT is limited to 4 bits, 0..15).

SIMPLE LAYER KEYS — base | layer:
- MO(n)  Momentary on while held:    base QK_MOMENTARY      = 0x5220 -> value = 0x5220 + n   (n 0..31, max range 0x523F)
- TG(n)  Toggle layer on/off:        base QK_TOGGLE_LAYER   = 0x5260 -> value = 0x5260 + n   (n 0..31, max 0x527F)
- TO(n)  Activate layer (turn off others in its range): base QK_TO = 0x5200 -> value = 0x5200 + n (n 0..31, max 0x521F)
- OSL(n) One-shot layer (next keypress): base QK_ONE_SHOT_LAYER = 0x5280 -> value = 0x5280 + n (n 0..31, max 0x529F)
- DF(n)  Set default layer:          base QK_DEF_LAYER      = 0x5240 -> value = 0x5240 + n   (n 0..31, max 0x525F)
- TT(n)  Layer-tap-toggle:           base QK_LAYER_TAP_TOGGLE = 0x52C0 -> value = 0x52C0 + n (n 0..31, max 0x52DF)

So to build these for layers 0..N: value = base + layer. Hex: e.g. MO(1)=0x5221, TG(2)=0x5262, TO(0)=0x5200, OSL(3)=0x5283.

COMPOSITE KEYS (layer + tap keycode), bit-packed, NOT a simple add:
- LT(layer, kc)  Layer-tap: hold = momentary layer, tap = send kc.
    base QK_LAYER_TAP = 0x4000 (range to 0x4FFF). Encoding: value = 0x4000 | ((layer & 0x0F) << 8) | (kc & 0xFF).
    layer is only 4 bits here (0..15); kc is a BASIC keycode (its low byte, e.g. KC_SPACE=0x2C). Example: LT(1, KC_SPACE) = 0x4000 | (1<<8) | 0x2C = 0x412C.
- LM(layer, mod)  Layer-mod (momentary layer + held mods): base QK_LAYER_MOD = 0x5000 (range to 0x51FF). Encoding: value = 0x5000 | ((layer & 0x0F) << 5) | (mod & 0x1F), where mod is the 5-bit MOD_MASK bitfield (bit0=LCTL,1=LSFT,2=LALT,3=LGUI; bit4 set = right-hand).

MODIFIER-WRAPPED KEYS (not layer, but same bit-pack idea, for completeness):
- Modded basic keycode (e.g. S(kc), LCTL(kc)): base QK_MODS = 0x0100. value = 0x0100 | ((mod & 0x1F) << 8) | (kc & 0xFF). e.g. S(KC_1)=0x0100|(0x02<<8)|0x1E=0x021E ('!'). Right-hand mods set bit4 (0x10) of the mod field.
- Mod-tap MT(mod, kc): base QK_MOD_TAP = 0x2000. value = 0x2000 | ((mod & 0x1F) << 8) | (kc & 0xFF).

All bases verified against /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk/quantum/keycodes.h lines 33-54.

lighting.via_channel: id_qmk_rgb_matrix_channel = 3 (defined in quantum/via.h:111, enum via_channel_id). Routed in quantum/via.c:243-248 → via_qmk_rgb_matrix_command(), which is implemented at quantum/via.c:637.

values.name: brightness
values.notes: via.h:129; value_id=1. GET (via.c:668-671): value_data[0] = rgb_matrix_get_val()*255/RGB_MATRIX_MAXIMUM_BRIGHTNESS. SET (via.c:694-696): rgb_matrix_sethsv_noeeprom(hue,sat, scale8(value_data[0], RGB_MATRIX_MAXIMUM_BRIGHTNESS)). On V1 Max RGB_MATRIX_MAXIMUM_BRIGHTNESS is not overridden, so it = UINT8_MAX = 255 (quantum/rgb_matrix/rgb_matrix.h:56) → the scale is effectively 1:1, full 0-255 range. Single data byte d0.

values.name: effect
values.notes: via.h:130; value_id=2. Single data byte d0 = effect index. SET (via.c:698-705): d0==0 → rgb_matrix_disable_noeeprom(); else rgb_matrix_enable_noeeprom() + rgb_matrix_mode_noeeprom(d0). GET (via.c:672-674): returns rgb_matrix_is_enabled() ? rgb_matrix_get_mode() : 0. NOTE: index 0 doubles as the enable/disable toggle (there is NO separate 'enable' value id on this channel). Range 0-22 per the VIA dropdown options in via_json/v1_max_ansi_encoder.json:23-47 (None=0 .. Solid Splash=22).

values.name: speed
values.notes: via.h:131; value_id=3. Single data byte d0. GET (via.c:676-678): rgb_matrix_get_speed(). SET (via.c:707-709): rgb_matrix_set_speed_noeeprom(d0). VIA shows this only when effect>1 (via_json line 50).

values.name: color
values.notes: via.h:132; value_id=4. TWO data bytes: d0=hue, d1=sat (each 0-255). GET (via.c:680-683): value_data[0]=rgb_matrix_get_hue(), value_data[1]=rgb_matrix_get_sat(). SET (via.c:711-713): rgb_matrix_sethsv_noeeprom(d0_hue, d1_sat, rgb_matrix_get_val()). Brightness(val) is preserved from current state; only hue+sat are set here. VIA shows color only for color-capable effects (via_json line 57).

lighting.set_format:
RAW HID custom-value protocol. Outer report dispatch in quantum/via.c:384-389 (id_custom_set_value=0x07, id_custom_get_value=0x08, id_custom_save=0x09) → via_custom_value_command (via.c:225) → routes by channel byte data[1] to via_qmk_rgb_matrix_command (via.c:637).

Byte layout of the HID payload (per comment at via.c:638 and 663):
  payload = [ command_id, channel_id, value_id, data0, data1, ... ]
    byte0 command_id : 0x07 set / 0x08 get / 0x09 save
    byte1 channel_id : 0x03  (id_qmk_rgb_matrix_channel)
    byte2 value_id   : 1=brightness, 2=effect, 3=speed, 4=color
    byte3.. value_data

Per value_id (data starts at byte3):
  brightness (id=1): [0x07,0x03,0x01,  val]                  val 0-255 (scale8 against max=255, i.e. 1:1)
  effect     (id=2): [0x07,0x03,0x02,  mode]                 mode 0-22; 0 disables matrix, 1-22 enable+select
  speed      (id=3): [0x07,0x03,0x03,  speed]                speed 0-255
  color      (id=4): [0x07,0x03,0x04,  hue, sat]             hue=byte3 0-255, sat=byte4 0-255 (val preserved)

GET mirrors the layout: send [0x08,0x03,value_id] and the firmware fills value_data in-place starting at byte3 (brightness→[d0], effect→[d0], speed→[d0], color→[d0=hue,d1=sat]) — see via.c:662-686.

SAVE/commit to EEPROM: send [0x09,0x03] (no value_id needed) → via_qmk_rgb_matrix_save (via.c:718). All set ops use *_noeeprom so changes are RAM-only until id_custom_save (0x09) is sent. Note save() only persists the single value_id that was most recently/exclusively set (it checks rgb_matrix_value_id_mask == single-bit, via.c:720-741), then clears the mask.

Byte→meaning mappings:
  brightness: byte value v (0-255) → val = scale8(v, RGB_MATRIX_MAXIMUM_BRIGHTNESS); since max=255 on V1 Max, val=v.
  hue:  byte value 0-255 maps directly to HSV hue (8-bit).
  sat:  byte value 0-255 maps directly to HSV saturation (8-bit).
  speed: byte value 0-255 maps directly to rgb_matrix speed.
  effect: byte value = QMK rgb_matrix mode number; 0 = matrix off.
lighting.notes:
Repo: /Users/vfi/keychron-qmk. Key files: quantum/via.h (enums), quantum/via.c (handlers), keyboards/keychron/v1_max/info.json (enabled animations), keyboards/keychron/v1_max/via_json/v1_max_ansi_encoder.json (authoritative VIA effect list + channel/value mapping).

CHANNEL CONSTANT: quantum/via.h:111 — id_qmk_rgb_matrix_channel = 3.
VALUE-ID ENUM: quantum/via.h:128-133 — via_qmk_rgb_matrix_value { brightness=1, effect=2, effect_speed=3, color=4 }.
COMMAND IDs: quantum/via.h:81-83 — id_custom_set_value=0x07, id_custom_get_value=0x08, id_custom_save=0x09.

IMPORTANT NUANCES:
1. There is NO separate 'enable'/'toggle' value id on the QMK RGB matrix channel for this firmware. Enable/disable is folded into the effect value (id=2): writing 0 disables the matrix, writing 1-22 enables and selects the mode (via.c:698-705). Likewise GET effect returns 0 when disabled (via.c:673). So treat 'effect' as also being the on/off control.

2. The 'color' value sets hue+sat only; brightness(val) is kept from current state (via.c:712). Two payload bytes: hue then sat.

3. Brightness scale: RGB_MATRIX_MAXIMUM_BRIGHTNESS is NOT overridden in any V1 Max config (config.h, ansi_encoder/config.h, info.json all lack it), so it falls back to the QMK core default UINT8_MAX=255 (quantum/rgb_matrix/rgb_matrix.h:56). Therefore the get-side multiply (val*255/255) and set-side scale8(v,255) are both effectively identity → the wire value is a straight 0-255 brightness.

4. Effect enum source-of-truth: the VIA dropdown in via_json/v1_max_ansi_encoder.json:23-47 lists the 23 options (None=0..Solid Splash=22) with their exact numeric mode values — this is what the VIA app sends as the effect byte and matches the firmware's enabled animations in info.json:32-54 (band_spiral_val, breathing, cycle_all, cycle_left_right, cycle_out_in, cycle_out_in_dual, cycle_pinwheel, cycle_spiral, cycle_up_down, digital_rain, dual_beacon, jellybean_raindrops, pixel_rain, rainbow_beacon, rainbow_moving_chevron, solid_reactive_multinexus, solid_reactive_multiwide, solid_reactive_simple, solid_splash, splash, typing_heatmap) plus the always-present SOLID_COLOR(1) base effect.

5. Keychron also compiles 2 CUSTOM effects (RGB_MATRIX_CUSTOM_KB=yes; PER_KEY_RGB and MIXED_RGB in keyboards/keychron/common/rgb/rgb_matrix_kb.inc:22-23, rgb.mk). These exist in the firmware mode enum AFTER the built-in effects but are NOT exposed in the standard VIA dropdown (used internally for retail demo / per-key custom lighting via a separate Keychron raw-HID path in common/rgb/keychron_rgb.c). They are not selectable via the standard id_qmk_rgb_matrix_effect dropdown shown above.

6. Persistence: all sets are *_noeeprom; you must send id_custom_save (0x09) on channel 3 to persist, and save() only writes the one field whose value_id was set since the last save (via.c:718-743).

ISO/JIS encoder variants share the identical channel/value protocol; only productId and physical layout differ (via_json/v1_max_iso_encoder.json, v1_max_jis_encoder.json).
